name: Chandrayaan-1
fact: India's first lunar mission. Carried NASA's Moon Mineralogy Mapper (M³) — which in 2009 returned the first direct spectroscopic confirmation of water (H₂O and OH) widely distributed across the lunar surface, not just at the poles. Mission ended after 312 days when communications were lost; M³ data is still being analysed today.
capability: First direct global confirmation of lunar surface water; transformed every subsequent lunar resource-utilisation roadmap.
